title: rubus horridus var. calyculatus
intro: Rubus horridus var. calyculatus, commonly known as the thimbleberry, is a type of bramble plant native to North America, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and roots are used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to their high content of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Rubus horridus var. calyculatus include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
